title: erigeron sylvaticus
intro: Erigeron sylvaticus, also known as wood's rosin root or rosin root, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its hairy, downy stems and small white or pink flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Erigeron sylvaticus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Erigeron sylvaticus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the dried roots being commonly used in herbal preparations.
